pains.1.text: You want a home in Armenia but fear prices might drop instead of rise
pains.2.title: Tired of agents and fake listing photos
pains.2.text: Paying 3–5% extra for outdated listings with photoshopped interiors
pains.3.title: You don't know where to invest
pains.3.text: You want property with growth potential, but everyone pitches the same Yerevan center listings
mechanism.title: Why Most Buyers Get It Wrong
mechanism.text: Most buyers look only at today's price — not tomorrow's infrastructure. Abovyan is on the verge of a tourism boom: the Christ monument on Mount Atis will make it a magnet for all of Armenia and the diaspora. Landmarks like this raise property values for kilometers around — and it's already happening.
consequences.title: What Happens If You Wait
consequences.1: Prices will rise before and after the monument opens
consequences.2: The best view-facing units will sell first
consequences.3: You'll miss buying directly from the owner with no markup
solution.title: This Apartment Is the Answer
solution.intro: 78 m² of spacious layout on the 4th floor of a 5-story building. Quiet green neighborhood, real views, honest photos.
spec.view: 🏔 View of green hills and Mount Atis
spec.condition: 🔧 Needs cosmetic renovation — blank canvas for your design
building.title: A Home with History — Built to Last
building.1.title: 1960s–70s Industrial-Era Construction
building.1.text: Classic Soviet institutional housing in industrial Abovyan: solid walls, generous layouts, herringbone parquet — a strong foundation for modern renovation.
building.2.title: Elar Quarter
building.2.text: Garni Street sits in historic Elar — established infrastructure with schools, shops, and transport within walking distance.
building.3.title: Private Ownership
building.3.text: Fully privatized — a standard private sale directly from the owner, no agent fees.
distances.title: Everything Nearby — On Foot
distances.center: City center (Andranik St.): ~500–600 m, 6–7 min walk
distances.transport: Bus stop: ~350 m, 4–5 min (Sevan / Pioneerakan St.)
distances.shop: Grocery stores: 150–200 m, 2 min · Carrefour / Garun: ~500 m, 5–6 min
location.title: Abovyan — A Tourism Hub with Massive Growth Potential
location.subtitle: A fast-growing Yerevan suburb where property is still affordable
nearby.atis.title: Christ Monument on Mount Atis
nearby.atis.text: A monumental statue of Jesus Christ (~77 m with pedestal) is being completed atop Mount Atis. The project will turn the area into an international pilgrimage and tourism destination — think Rio. Property values here rise automatically.
nearby.adventure.title: Adventure & Eco-Tourism
nearby.adventure.text: Garni Adventure Park offers enduro, ATV, and buggy tours through Kotayk's mountain trails. Eco-hotels and ethno-villages are launching nearby for high-spending tourists.
nearby.church.title: St. John the Baptist Church
nearby.church.text: A majestic modern church built from rare tuff — one of Armenia's most beautiful. Tour buses arrive daily from across the country.
nearby.logistics.title: Perfect Logistics
nearby.logistics.text: Mountain air + 10–15 minutes to Yerevan. Perfect for tourist rentals or permanent living.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.1.a: Yes. We'll do a video tour and help with paperwork. Many diaspora buyers complete the purchase remotely via power of attorney.
faq.2.a: Livable but needs cosmetic renovation. Parquet, walls, utilities — solid Soviet-era bones ready for a modern makeover.
faq.3.a: The price reflects the tourism-growth location, 78 m² size, and zero agent fees. Open to negotiation for a quick deal.
faq.4.q: Are there other units for sale in the building?
faq.4.a: No. This is the only apartment for sale in the building — direct from the owner.
faq.5.q: When was the building constructed? What does "factory housing" mean?
faq.5.a: A 5-story institutional housing block from the 1960s–70s in the Elar quarter. In Abovyan, such buildings were erected for industrial workers — valued for solid construction and spacious layouts. Today it's a standard privatized apartment.
